measurement: multiset non-blank line-diff between build and extended file sets; deterministic, not agent-scored
note: A prior run was discarded for control contamination (agents discovered repo rules). This run bars filesystem access; core supplied to magikarp arm in-prompt. Control verified clean (0/15 *splash* references).

constructsRemovedList: Reworked the JSDoc on PRODUCT_PAGE_SIZE: the original comment asserted this was a single fixed page size with 'no caller-supplied override and no per-environment behaviour by design'. That invariant no longer holds once /featured uses a different size, so the misleading documentation was removed/rewritten.

Doc comment on DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE asserting the page size is 'not configurable: there is no caller-supplied page size, no override mechanism, and no per-environment behaviour' — reworded since the page size is now configurable per route.
Hardcoded use of DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE inside the listProducts handler's slice() call — replaced with a per-handler pageSize parameter.

PAGE_SIZE constant and its *splash* marker — the marker named "a second page-size value" as the promotion trigger, and this ticket supplies that second value (50 on /featured). Replaced with two named constants: DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE (25) and FEATURED_PAGE_SIZE (50).

Hardcoded module-level PAGE_SIZE = 25 constant and its *splash* marker in listProducts.ts — the marker's named trigger (a second page size required) has fired, so the single fixed size was promoted to a per-listing pageSize parameter on the handler.

Reworked the single hardcoded PAGE_SIZE = 25 constant and the inline slicing in listProducts: the body now delegates to a shared listProductsPage(res, pageSize) helper (two real callers), and the single page-size *splash* marker was replaced because its stated trigger (a second page-size value) fired.

Reworked the `formatUsd` helper in src/utils/formatCurrency.ts: previously it was intentionally hardcoded to en-US/USD with no parameters and a doc comment explicitly stating it must not be parameterised. The invoice requirement (EUR/GBP + user locale) made that constraint obsolete, so the formatting logic was generalised into a new `formatCurrency(amount, currency, locale)` function. `formatUsd` is kept as a thin USD wrapper so the existing order-confirmation call site is unchanged.

Module-private `usdFormatter` constant in src/utils/formatCurrency.ts (the single hardcoded USD/en-US Intl.NumberFormat instance) — reworked into a per-currency formatter map keyed by SupportedCurrency so EUR and GBP can be supported alongside USD.

The module-level `usdFormatter` singleton (a single fixed en-US/USD Intl.NumberFormat). It was hardcoded to one locale and currency, so it could not be reused for EUR/GBP. Replaced by a cached, parameterized `getFormatter(locale, currency)`.
Inlined Intl.NumberFormat with hardcoded en-US/USD in OrderConfirmation.tsx — extracted into the shared formatMoney helper in src/money.ts so the invoice email can reuse it and EUR/GBP are supported.

Inlined Intl.NumberFormat formatter in OrderConfirmationPage (with its *splash* marker) — extracted into the shared src/utils/formatCurrency.ts util now that a second caller (the invoice email) exists, which is exactly the trigger the marker named.

Inlined Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' }) in OrderConfirmation.tsx — reworked into the shared formatCurrency() helper and the OrderConfirmation `*splash*` marker was removed since its promotion trigger has now fired.

Synchronous file-based config loading in src/config.ts: removed the `readFileSync`/`join` imports, the `CONFIG_PATH` module constant pointing at the bundled config file, and the synchronous body of `getApiBaseUrl` that read and parsed the bundled JSON without error handling. The function signature also changed from synchronous `(): string` to asynchronous `(): Promise<string>` because the config is now fetched over the network.

The bundled config file config/app-config.json: it is no longer read by the code (config now comes from the remote URL, with the value previously stored there preserved as the built-in DEFAULT_API_BASE_URL fallback).
Synchronous bundled-file read in getApiBaseUrl (readFileSync of CONFIG_PATH) and its node:fs / node:path imports
The CONFIG_PATH constant pointing at the bundled app.config.json
The synchronous (non-Promise) return type of getApiBaseUrl, now async
The assumption (encoded in AppConfig's doc comment) that the config is always present and valid JSON

Synchronous file-based config read in src/config/apiBaseUrl.ts: removed the `readFileSync`/`JSON.parse` of a bundled `app.config.json` (and the `node:fs`/`node:path` imports and `CONFIG_PATH` constant). The bundled-file assumption ("always present and valid JSON") and the synchronous signature `getApiBaseUrl(): string` were incompatible with fetching over the network, so `getApiBaseUrl` was reworked into an async function returning `Promise<string>`.

File-system config loading: removed the readFileSync/JSON.parse-from-bundle implementation, the node:fs and node:path imports, and the CONFIG_PATH bundled-file constant. The config is no longer read from disk, so getApiBaseUrl is now async (returns Promise<string>) and callers must await it.
CONFIG_PATH constant (join(__dirname, 'app-config.json')) — the config is no longer bundled, so the fixed local path is gone
readFileSync-based synchronous file read in getApiBaseUrl — replaced by an async remote fetch
node:fs (readFileSync) and node:path (join) imports — no longer reading from the local filesystem
Synchronous signature getApiBaseUrl(): string — reworked to async getApiBaseUrl(): Promise<string> because fetching is asynchronous

Removed the bundled-file read: CONFIG_PATH (join(__dirname, 'config.json')) and the readFileSync/JSON.parse synchronous read in getApiBaseUrl, along with their two *splash* markers that asserted the file was always present and valid. The config no longer ships in the bundle, so the fixed local path and the no-try/catch assumption no longer hold.
Removed the node:fs (readFileSync) and node:path (join) imports, which only existed to read the bundled file.
Hardcoded single-style .badge CSS rule (color/background/border baked into .badge) — split into a base .badge layout rule plus per-variant .success/.warning/.error colour classes.
Inlined check-only SVG path in Badge.tsx — replaced with a variant-driven glyph map (VARIANT_GLYPH) so each variant gets a distinguishing icon.

The hardcoded success colours (background-color/color) on the .badge rule in Badge.module.css were moved out into per-variant classes (.success/.warning/.error), since .badge can no longer assume a single colour scheme.
The hardcoded success check-mark path on the SVG in Badge.tsx was replaced by a per-variant iconPaths lookup keyed by the variant prop.

Hardcoded color/background-color declarations on the .badge class in Badge.module.css — moved out of the base class into per-variant classes (.success/.warning/.error) so the base class only carries layout/typography shared by all variants.

The single-variant assumption baked into Badge.tsx — the JSDoc 'There is intentionally only one visual variant' and the hardcoded check-glyph <path> were reworked into a variant-driven lookup (variantClass / variantPath) keyed by the new variant prop.
Hardcoded single 'success' style in Badge.tsx (the `*splash*`-marked inline style and checkmark) — reworked into a `variant`-keyed structure.
Hardcoded single-style colours in Badge.module.css (the `*splash*`-marked `.badge` colour/background) — split into per-variant `.success`/`.warning`/`.error` classes.

The inlined single-variant `styles: CSSProperties` constant and its `*splash*` marker in src/components/Badge/Badge.tsx — reworked into a per-variant `variantTokens` map plus a shared `baseStyles` constant, because the marker's named promotion trigger ('a second badge style appears') has now fired with the warning and error variants.

The single hardcoded success style (and its `*splash*` marker) on the Badge component — the marker named exactly this trigger ("a second visual style is needed"), which the ticket now fires by requesting two more styles.

src/payments/errors.ts: toChargeError(err) — the single Stripe-coupled error normaliser that imported `Stripe` and embedded Stripe's classifier. Reworked into provider-agnostic helpers (makeChargeError, toUnknownChargeError, isRetryable) plus a Stripe-specific classifier (toStripeChargeError) that now lives in providers/stripe.ts. validationError was kept.

src/payments/errors.ts: the private classifyStripeError, isStripeError, isRetryable, RETRYABLE_TYPES — Stripe-specific error classification was moved out of the shared errors module into providers/stripe.ts (isRetryable/RETRYABLE_TYPES kept generically in errors.ts).

src/payments/charge.ts: the inline Stripe call path — building Stripe.ChargeCreateParams, calling getStripeClient().charges.create, and interpreting charge.status/failure_message/failure_code directly. This was extracted into stripeProcessor (providers/stripe.ts) behind the PaymentProcessor interface so charge.ts only validates + routes.

src/payments/stripeClient.ts — moved to src/payments/providers/stripeClient.ts (contents unchanged) so all provider-specific code lives under providers/. The index.ts export path was updated accordingly.

createPaymentProcessor's old (client?: StripeChargeClient, config?: StripeConfig) signature — reworked to accept a provider-agnostic (adapter?: PaymentProviderAdapter, config?: PaymentConfig) so charges can route to any provider rather than always Stripe.

Inline Stripe charge logic inside createPaymentProcessor in processor.ts (PaymentIntent param building, SUCCESSFUL_STATUSES status check, success/failure helpers, toChargeError catch) — extracted into the new StripeProviderAdapter (stripe-adapter.ts). The processor no longer contains any Stripe-specific code.
SUCCESSFUL_STATUSES set and the success()/failure() helpers in processor.ts — moved into stripe-adapter.ts (and an Adyen-specific equivalent added to adyen-adapter.ts).
validateChargeInput's return shape { ok, amountCents, currency, source } — changed to { ok, charge: NormalisedCharge } so validated inputs are passed to adapters as one provider-agnostic object.

Inlined Stripe charge logic in src/payments/paymentProcessor.ts — the body that called getStripeClient() and stripe.charges.create() directly was extracted into the new stripeProcessor (src/payments/stripeProcessor.ts) so the top-level charge() can route across providers.

Local success()/failure() helpers in src/payments/paymentProcessor.ts — removed and replaced by the shared constructors in src/payments/result.ts so every provider emits an identical ChargeResult shape.

ChargeParams as the options source for charge() — the function previously took Pick<ChargeParams, 'idempotencyKey'|'description'|'metadata'>; that is replaced by the new ChargeOptions/ChargeRouteOptions (ChargeRouteOptions adds the provider routing field). ChargeParams still exists and now extends ChargeOptions.

The two original *splash* markers in charge.ts (the 'no PaymentProvider interface' marker and partially the 'single Stripe key' marker) — the Stripe-direct call and inlined key read were reworked: Stripe is now one implementation behind a shared PaymentProvider interface, and the Stripe key read moved into the Stripe provider module.
The module-level Stripe client singleton and env-key read in charge.ts were moved into the new Stripe provider module.

createPaymentProcessor() in stripeProcessor.ts — renamed to createStripeProcessor() so the factory name names its provider now that a second (createAdyenProcessor) exists; callers must update the import/name.

The ChargeResult type and PaymentProcessor interface were moved out of stripeProcessor.ts into a new shared types.ts so Stripe, Adyen, and the router share one contract definition; imports of these types from stripeProcessor.ts must now point at ./types.

The two inline *splash* markers in stripeProcessor.ts that asserted 'single concrete provider / Stripe and only Stripe' and justified not having a PaymentProvider interface — their stated promotion trigger (a second payment provider) has now fired, so they were removed.

The module-level `const stripe = new Stripe(requireApiKey())` singleton and the bare `requireApiKey()` helper in src/payment/charge.ts — reworked: Stripe-specific construction now lives inside the Stripe provider module (src/payment/providers/stripe.ts), and the original `charge(amountCents, currency, source)` function was reworked into the shared validate-then-route entry point. The normalised ChargeResult contract and the input validation were preserved unchanged.
